system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      decomposeParDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

numberOfSubdomains  4;

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

vertices
(

    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5

    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - transition from bend to outlet (going toward negative x)
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight section (from x=25 to x=-500)

    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x: -50 -> 0)
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section (x: 0 -> 25)
    hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Transition from bend to outlet (x: 25 -> 25 but going toward outlet)
    hex (5 6 10 9 7 8 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet straight section (x: 25 -> -500)
    hex (12 13 16 17 14 15 18 19) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall

            // Block 1 top face (z = +25 plane)

            // Block 2 bottom face (z = -25 plane)
            (5 6 10 9)
            // Block 3 bottom face (z = -25 plane)
            (14 15 18 19)
        );
    }

    frontWall

            // Block 1 front face (y = -50 plane)
            (11 3 2 10)
            // Block 2 front face (y = -75 plane)

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 1 has "inward-pointing faces"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(6 7 11 10)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The previous attempt correctly identified the winding issue and reversed all block windings. That fixed Blocks 0, 2, and 3, but now **Block 1** `(4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10)` has a face `(6 7 11 10)` that points inward. Let me carefully trace this.

First, let's understand the vertex positions:
- 4: (0, 25, 25), 5: (0, 25, -25), 9: (25, 0, -25), 8: (25, 0, 25)
- 6: (0, -25, -25), 7: (0, -25, 25), 11: (25, -50, 25), 10: (25, -50, -25)

Block 1 connects vertices at x=0 (face 4,5,6,7) to vertices at x=25 (face 8,9,10,11). So the first face is (4 5 9 8) and the second face is (6 7 11 10). The face `(6 7 11 10)` consists of:
- 6: (0, -25, -25), 7: (0, -25, 25), 11: (25, -50, 25), 10: (25, -50, -25)

Using the right-hand rule on (6→7→11): vectors are (0,0,50) and (25,-25,50). Cross product: (0·50-50·(-25), 50·25-0·50, 0·(-25)-0·25) = (1250, 1250, 0). This points roughly in +x/+y, but the issue is that the face orientation combined with the block geometry creates an inward-facing side relative to the intended block interior.

**Proposed Fix:**

The entire block definition for Block 1 needs to be reconsidered. The problem is that the face (6 7 11 10) winds such that its normal doesn't consistently point away from the block interior. Reverse the winding of **this single block**:

Change Block 1 from:
```
hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10) ...
```
to:
```
hex (4 9 5 8 6 11 7 10) ...
```

However, this is tricky because reversing just one block breaks the shared face consistency with adjacent blocks. A better approach: **reverse the winding of ALL blocks simultaneously AND swap the face definitions consistently**.

But the real issue is geometric: the bend geometry as currently defined creates non-convex or inverted hexahedra. The vertex at (25, -50) creates a sharp internal corner that makes Block 1 problematic. The solution is to **restructure the block connectivity entirely** to follow the squareBend tutorial pattern where the bend flows in +x and the outlet extends in -x, ensuring all blocks are convex and properly connected.

Specifically, redesign the blocks so that:
- Block 0: Connects inlet (x=-50) to bend apex (x=0) — already correct
- Block 1: Should connect the bend apex (x=0) to the far bend corner. The face shared between Block 0 and Block 1 is the x=0 face of Block 0 (vertices 4,5,6,7) and must match exactly with Block 1's x=0 face. Ensure all face windings are consistent across shared faces.

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cascading error

Resolves once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

The core problem across all attempts is that the **vertex positions combined with the chosen block connectivity create geometrically invalid (degenerate or inverted) hexahedra**, especially in the bend region. Simply reordering windings isn't sufficient — the block topology itself needs to be restructured to produce valid convex hexahedra. Study the canonical squareBend tutorial's blockMeshDict carefully and replicate its proven vertex numbering and block connection scheme, adapting only the coordinate values to match the provided geometry. Pay special attention to ensuring shared faces between blocks have identical (consistent) windings.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire blockMeshDict with corrected version that fixes inward-pointing faces in Block 1 by restructuring the block connectivity and vertex ordering to ensure all hexahedra are convex and share consistent face windings; Replace Block 0 with hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) connecting inlet to bend apex at x=0; Replace Block 1 with hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10) but reordered as hex (4 8 5 9 6 10 7 11) to fix the inward face issue while maintaining connection to Block 0 through face (4 5 6 7); Replace Block 2 with hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) but reordered appropriately to connect Block 1 to Block 3; Replace Block 3 with hex (12 13 16 17 14 15 18 19) keeping the long outlet section from x=25 to x=-500; Ensure all boundary faces maintain correct windings for inlet outet upperWall lowerWall frontWall backWall; Keep scale 0.001 and all vertex coordinates unchanged')]
